articles.headline: Trump threats against Iran proxies cast shadow over Geneva peace talks
articles.summary: President Donald Trump threatened to restart strikes on Iran for its support of Hezbollah militants, casting doubt on high-stakes peace negotiations in Geneva. Iran’s parliamentary speaker responded by stating their armed forces are prepared to act. Although talks briefly recessed due to the social media posts, mediators indicate the Iranian delegation remains engaged in the process.

articles.headline: Two killed after speeding BMW luxury car crashes in Badlapur
articles.summary: A man and a woman in their twenties were killed and one person was critically injured when their speeding BMW Z-series convertible crashed into a divider on an under-construction stretch of the Mumbai-Vadodara highway. The group was returning home from a birthday celebration when the vehicle flipped multiple times. Occupants were thrown from the car because the roof was retracted.

articles.headline: Over 2 million students appear for NEET-UG 2026 retest amid security
articles.summary: More than 2 million medical aspirants took the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination across 5,454 centres globally. The retest follows allegations of a paper leak during the original May 3 exam. Security was high, with a candidate arrested in Varanasi for possessing a SIM card and an old question paper. NTA officials monitored the process closely to ensure the integrity of the medical entrance test.

articles.headline: CBSE releases Class 12 re-evaluation results for 147,000 students
articles.summary: The CBSE announced re-evaluation results for approximately 147,000 Class 12 students, representing 87% of those who applied for verification. The board processed these results following the original May declaration. Revised marksheets are now available via DigiLocker. The remaining applications are expected to be processed in phases over the coming days.

articles.headline: Three India bound vessels safely transit Strait of Hormuz despite tensions
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Shipping confirmed that three bulk carriers bound for India—Equinox Orendra, Chang Chang Dong Hai, and Ze Hui—safely transited the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday. This comes despite Iran’s claims of closing the waterway. Ten Indian vessels currently remain in the Persian Gulf, with dozens more across the Gulf of Oman, Aden, and the Red Sea.

articles.headline: SIT likely to submit Ayodhya Ram Temple fund embezzlement report Monday
articles.summary: A special investigation team is expected to submit its preliminary report to UP CM Yogi Adityanath regarding the alleged embezzlement of donation funds at the Ram Temple. Investigating phased land purchases and building material procurement, the SIT has identified 150 suspects. Five individuals associated with counting donations have already been questioned, with recoveries totaling ₹2 crore.

articles.headline: Two Shiv Sena UBT lawmakers defect to Eknath Shinde led faction
articles.summary: Dharashiv MP Omraje Nimbalkar and Hingoli MP Nagesh Ashtikar formally announced their decision to join the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena. The move is a significant blow to Uddhav Thackeray's party, with four more rebel MPs expected to switch sides soon. Nimbalkar cited political survival and constituency development needs as primary reasons for the defection.

articles.headline: Ammonia gas leak at Tamil Nadu shrimp factory kills two workers
articles.summary: Two women died and 59 others were hospitalized following a suspected ammonia coolant leak at a private shrimp processing facility in Thiruvallur district. The incident occurred while workers were resting on a Sunday morning. Police have arrested the two owners of the factory. Chief Minister Vijay announced ₹2 lakh compensation for the families of the deceased.

articles.headline: Centre reappoints Tushar Mehta as Solicitor General for three years
articles.summary: The Appointments Committee of the Cabinet has extended the term of Solicitor General Tushar Mehta for another three years. Additionally, the government extended the tenures of five Additional Solicitor Generals—Vikramjeet Banerjee, KM Nataraj, SV Raju, N Venkataraman, and Aishwarya Bhati—to serve in the Supreme Court and Delhi High Court.

articles.headline: PM Modi commissions three indigenous naval vessels to boost maritime might
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi commissioned three indigenously built vessels—stealth frigate INS Dunagiri, anti-submarine craft INS Agray, and survey ship INS Sanshodhak—at the Syama Prasad Mookerjee Port. Built by GRSE, these ships represent India's push for self-reliance in the defense sector. Modi stated that India no longer wants to be a mere buyer in the international defense market.

articles.headline: Delhi govt plans blockchain system for easier utility transfers for property
articles.summary: The Delhi government is considering a single-window mechanism using blockchain technology to automatically transfer water and electricity connections when a property is registered. The proposed system aims to eliminate the need for buyers to visit multiple offices. Data from the directorate of survey and settlement will be shared with relevant agencies to streamline the process.

articles.headline: Differently abled man stabbed to death by neighbours in northwest Delhi
articles.summary: A 50-year-old differently abled man was killed in northwest Delhi following an altercation with his neighbours. Police have apprehended five individuals, including a minor, in connection with the stabbing. The incident occurred during a dispute, and the victim succumbed to his injuries shortly after. An investigation into the motive is currently underway.

articles.headline: Political row erupts in West Bengal over renaming Kolkata Suhrawardy Avenue
articles.summary: The Kolkata Municipal Corporation sparked controversy by renaming Suhrawardy Avenue as Gopal Mukherjee Road. BJP leaders hailed the move as a historic correction, noting Gopal Mukherjee's role in the 1947 riots. Opponents and academics criticized the decision, arguing that the road was originally named after academician Sir Hassan Suhrawardy and the change distorts Bengal's history.

articles.headline: Pressure mounts on British PM Keir Starmer to set resignation timetable
articles.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is reportedly considering his political future as popularity ratings hit record lows. Following a decisive election win for rival Andy Burnham, several ministers and dozens of lawmakers have privately called for Starmer to set a departure date. Starmer is currently discussing his position with family, with US President Trump also predicting his resignation.

articles.headline: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi hits record 11 ball half century
articles.summary: 15-year-old cricketer Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed the fastest half-century in List A history, reaching the milestone in just 11 balls. He eventually scored 94 runs off 29 balls against Sri Lanka A in a tri-nation series final in Dambulla. His performance, which included 10 fours and eight sixes, powered India A to a 66-run victory.

articles.headline: Expanded 48 team football World Cup defies predictions of lopsided results
articles.summary: The expanded 48-team FIFA World Cup is proving more competitive than critics predicted. Despite fears that smaller nations would lead to lopsided scores, teams like Cape Verde held European champions Spain to a draw. Goal differentials remain consistent with previous 32-team tournaments, suggesting that global coaching and physical training standards have narrowed the gap between nations.

articles.headline: Karnataka BJP to hold legislature meeting at temple over cross voting
articles.summary: State BJP president BY Vijayendra announced that the party will convene its legislature meeting at the Sri Manjunatheshwara Temple in Dharmasthala. The move aims to address allegations that at least four MLAs cross-voted during recent MLC elections. Legislators may be asked to take an oath before the deity to establish the truth of their voting conduct.

articles.headline: Europe braces for record temperatures as severe heatwave creates heat dome
articles.summary: A mass of hot Saharan air has created a 'heat dome' over western and central Europe, pushing temperatures toward 40°C. The 'African anticyclone' system is trapping heat, leading to nationwide weather warnings and concerns over infrastructure strain. Meteorologists warn the phenomenon will allow temperatures to build over several days, affecting wildlife and tourism hotspots.

articles.headline: Armed Nihang group locks themselves in Rudraprayag gurdwara over arrests
articles.summary: A group of Nihangs armed with spears and swords stormed a gurdwara in Rudraprayag, Uttarakhand, locking themselves inside. They are demanding the release of four sect members arrested after a clash in Chamoli. Police have surrounded the building while the group remains on the terrace, blocking staircase access to the upper floors.

articles.headline: CAG report reveals declining tax-GSDP ratios across major Indian states
articles.summary: A new CAG report covering 2015 to 2025 shows that 12 out of 18 large Indian states have seen their State Own Tax Revenue (SOTR) to GSDP ratio decline. Bihar and Tamil Nadu recorded the steepest falls. The report highlights that while GST has constrained state autonomy, many states have also struggled to mobilize non-GST revenues like excise and stamp duties.

articles.headline: JD(S) launches foot march against land acquisition for Bidadi township project
articles.summary: The JD(S) Youth Wing, led by Nikhil Kumaraswamy, began an 11-km foot march named 'Namma Bhoomi, Namma Hakku' to protest land acquisition for the Greater Bengaluru Integrated Township. Farmers and villagers joined the march, expressing fears that the 7,481-acre project will destroy livelihoods. The party accused the government of using brokers to break farmer unity.

articles.headline: Karnataka to introduce smart cards for free bus travel scheme
articles.summary: The Karnataka Transport Department is preparing to issue smart cards to Shakti scheme beneficiaries to curb fraud. The move follows reports of passengers using fake Aadhaar cards to claim free travel. Applicants will need to verify their eligibility using voter IDs and Aadhaar. The cards will be non-transferable and may later be integrated with the Namma Metro system.

articles.headline: B K Hariprasad formally assumes charge as Karnataka Congress president
articles.summary: Senior OBC leader B K Hariprasad took over as the Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee president from outgoing chief D K Shivakumar. The ceremony was attended by AICC president Mallikarjun Kharge and CM Siddaramaiah. Hariprasad, a former Rajya Sabha member, is tasked with preparing the party for upcoming local body elections and the 2028 Assembly polls.

articles.headline: Decomposed headless body of man found in locked Raichur house
articles.summary: Raichur police discovered the headless, decomposed body of a 35-year-old man named Tirupati inside a locked rented house. Neighbours alerted authorities after noticing a foul smell. Investigators believe the murder occurred several days prior and that the killers removed the head from the scene. The victim had a history of criminal cases, and police are investigating potential rivalries.

articles.headline: Bengaluru woman falls to death while clearing overgrown tree branches
articles.summary: A 58-year-old woman, Pramila Bai, died after falling from her terrace in Rajajinagar. She was attempting to push away overgrown tree branches with a mop handle when the sheet she was standing on gave way. Her family alleged that civic authorities had repeatedly ignored requests to prune the tree. Forest officials denied receiving any formal complaints regarding the property.

articles.headline: AMMA governing council resigns in Kerala amid infighting and financial row
articles.summary: The entire 17-member governing council of the Association of Malayalam Movie Artists, including president Shweta Menon, resigned during a general body meeting. The mass resignation follows allegations of financial mismanagement and internal disputes. Menon, the first woman to lead the body, claimed there was a deliberate agenda to remove her and denied any accounting irregularities.

articles.headline: Six arrested after Odisha native beaten to death in Kerala
articles.summary: Police in Thrissur arrested six individuals, including four women, for the murder of 27-year-old Dhanpati Nayak from Odisha. Nayak was allegedly assaulted at a rented house where an illegal interstate prostitution racket was being operated. The altercation reportedly started over a monetary dispute. The victim died at a government hospital due to severe head injuries.

articles.headline: Political parties in Tamil Nadu oppose amendment to Mekedatu dam resolution
articles.summary: Opposition parties including the AIADMK, CPI(M), and PMK have raised objections to an amendment in the Tamil Nadu assembly's resolution against the Mekedatu dam. The amendment, proposed by CM Joseph Vijay, suggests urging the Centre to form a new tribunal. Critics argue that a new tribunal would complicate the issue and ignore existing legal rulings that already favor Tamil Nadu.

articles.headline: Controversy intensifies over Kerala government proposal to cut low alcohol taxes
articles.summary: The UDF government in Kerala is facing backlash over a budget proposal to reduce taxes on low-alcohol beverages. Veteran Congress leader VM Sudheeran urged the Chief Minister to withdraw the plan, stating it encourages liquor consumption and violates election manifesto promises. The opposition CPI(M) also criticized the move, alleging it was designed to benefit private liquor companies.

articles.headline: Kerala reports one more Shigella death and seven fresh infection cases
articles.summary: Health authorities in Kerala confirmed one more death and seven new cases of Shigella, bringing the total fatalities in June to six. The latest victim was a 54-year-old woman from Kozhikode. Currently, 140 cases have been reported this month across multiple districts. Officials are urging strict hygiene and caution as the bacterial infection continues to spread through contaminated water.

articles.headline: BJP workers protest in Kerala over alleged assault at Christian facility
articles.summary: Tensions rose in Omallur as BJP activists protested outside a Christian worship centre following allegations that staff assaulted a 17-year-old boy. The boy had reportedly been made to work at the facility instead of receiving promised educational support. Police filed cases against 30 protesters for manhandling believers, while three staff members were arrested under the Juvenile Justice Act.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh forms SIT to probe alleged police custodial murder case
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh government has constituted a Special Investigation Team to investigate the alleged illegal detention and murder of Gade Sai Krishna. Krishna’s family accused a now-suspended police inspector of torturing him to death and clandestinely cremating the body. The SIT, led by IG M Ravi Prakash, has been directed to conduct a transparent and expeditious investigation.

articles.headline: Mallikarjun Kharge reprimands Congress workers for chanting individual slogans
articles.summary: During a party event in Bengaluru, Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge rebuked workers for chanting slogans in support of D K Shivakumar. Calling them 'useless fellows,' Kharge emphasized that the gathering was a party programme, not an individual’s event. He questioned the decorum of the attendees and stated that individual worship has no place in the collective party mission.

articles.headline: Odisha Assembly Speaker rejects BJD plea to disqualify eight rebel MLAs
articles.summary: Odisha Speaker Surama Padhy dismissed a Biju Janata Dal petition seeking the disqualification of eight rebel legislators. The MLAs were accused of defying the party whip by cross-voting for a BJP-backed candidate during the Rajya Sabha elections in March. The Speaker ruled that the petitions failed to comply with mandatory procedural requirements for disqualification.

articles.headline: Nitish Kumar says he will dedicate more time to JD(U) organisation
articles.summary: During a JD(U) national council meeting in Patna, Bihar CM Nitish Kumar announced he would spend more time on organizational work to expand the party's footprint. The meeting focused on strengthening the party's foundation ahead of future elections. Kumar expressed his commitment to working toward a 'prosperous and developed Bihar' through unified party efforts.

articles.headline: Manipur Kuki Naga violence escalates after six Nagas killed by militants
articles.summary: The killing of six Naga civilians, including a priest, by suspected Kuki militants has raised fears of a return to the 1990s civil war in Manipur. The incident followed a series of abductions and a drunken brawl in Ukhrul district. Community leaders and analysts warn that land disputes and ethnic boundary mapping are fueling a new wave of inter-community friction and demographic imbalance.

articles.headline: Prime Minister Modi delays airport departure to avoid inconveniencing NEET students
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chose to remain at Delhi airport for extra time on Sunday to prevent his security convoy from creating traffic delays for students traveling to NEET-UG examination centres. Officials noted that the PM arrived from Kolkata but delayed his departure until the 2 PM exam start time to ensure medical aspirants faced no transit hurdles in the national capital.

articles.headline: Candidate caught using mobile phone during NEET re-test in Jaipur
articles.summary: A student appearing for the NEET-UG re-examination in Jaipur was caught using a mobile phone hidden under her clothing. The device was connected to her shirt buttons to evade metal detectors. An invigilator noticed suspicious activity, leading to the student's detention. Preliminary inquiries revealed this was her third attempt at the medical entrance test.

articles.headline: Maoist free Abujhmad undergoes first formal land survey since Independence
articles.summary: In Konge village, located in Chhattisgarh’s Abujhmad region, the government has launched the first formal revenue land survey since Independence. The area, long a Maoist stronghold, had no official demarcation of private land. Revenue officials are mapping parcels and assigning unique Khasra numbers to establish tribal land rights, marking a shift toward regular state administration.

articles.headline: Mother and son drown in Barmer farm pond while taking selfies
articles.summary: A tragic accident in Rajasthan’s Barmer district resulted in the death of a 30-year-old woman and her 7-year-old son. The two drowned in a farm pond in Bano Ki Beri Doodh village while attempting to take selfies. The incident occurred on Sunday morning, and police confirmed the mother died while trying to rescue her son after he fell into the water.

articles.headline: Six killed and 27 injured in West Bengal bus-truck collision
articles.summary: A government bus traveling from Siliguri to Cooch Behar crashed into a stationary truck in Jalpaiguri district, killing six people including an 18-month-old boy. The accident, which occurred during adverse weather and slippery road conditions, left 27 others injured. Police have detained the bus driver and are investigating the exact cause of the fatal collision.

articles.headline: Uttarakhand mandates prior permission for filming at Char Dham shrines
articles.summary: The Uttarakhand government has introduced new regulations requiring social media influencers, vloggers, and filmmakers to obtain official permission before shooting content at Char Dham shrines. The move aims to manage the conduct of digital creators within temple premises. This follows a surge in viral content that authorities believe needs better regulation to maintain religious sanctity.

articles.headline: Odisha minister’s nephew booked for abetment to suicide of engineering student
articles.summary: Police have registered a case of abetment to suicide against the nephew of Odisha Transport Minister Bibhuti Bhushan Jena. The action follows the death of a 21-year-old engineering student who was found dead in her hostel room near Berhampur. The investigation was launched after the student's family raised allegations against the high-profile relative.

articles.headline: BJP chief Nitin Nabin targets Punjab CM Mann over sacrilege row
articles.summary: BJP national president Nitin Nabin criticized Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann, stating that the people would not tolerate disrespect toward Sikh gurus following a recent sacrilege video controversy. During a visit to Ludhiana, Nabin also accused the AAP government of misusing ₹1.87 lakh crore in central funds provided for Punjab’s development over the last decade.

articles.headline: Trump proposes renaming ICE to NICE to reshape agency perception
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump floated a plan on social media to rename Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to 'NICE' (National Immigration and Customs Enforcement). Trump argued that rebranding the agency would confuse journalists and help combat negative media portrayals of its enforcement operations. He described the agents as 'great patriots' facing a hostile environment.

articles.headline: Netanyahu says Israeli troops will remain in Lebanon as long as necessary
articles.summary: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu declared that Israeli forces will stay in the southern Lebanon security zone as long as needed to protect northern residents. He also vowed to prevent Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons, regardless of political shifts. Concurrently, Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem rejected any permanent Israeli presence, labeling Israel an aggressor that must exit Lebanese land.

articles.headline: Centre plans ₹7,100 crore chip incentives to attract fresh investments
articles.summary: India is set to disburse ₹7,100 crore this fiscal year under its semiconductor mission to support chip fabrication, manufacturing, and design. The move is expected to attract ₹15,000 crore in fresh investments and generate 4,700 jobs. Targeting a complete domestic ecosystem, the funding will support one fab unit, nine manufacturing facilities, and 30 design firms.

articles.headline: India plans outreach program to boost exports via trade agreements
articles.summary: The commerce ministry is launching a nationwide mission to help exporters utilize nine trade agreements covering 38 countries. Commerce secretary Rajesh Agrawal stated the initiative aims to deepen integration with global value chains. Despite global tensions, India’s total exports grew 14.6% in the first two months of the fiscal year, with a focus on reaching a $1 trillion goal.

articles.headline: Tata group’s Agratas signs $530 million EV battery deal with JLR
articles.summary: Agratas, the Tata group’s battery arm, has secured a seven-year supply agreement with Jaguar Land Rover worth $530 million. The contract will initially cover nickel manganese cobalt (NMC) battery cells starting this fiscal year. Agratas is currently constructing massive manufacturing plants in Gujarat and the UK to meet captive demand and support JLR's transition to an all-electric future.

articles.headline: Dalmia Bharat plans to raise ₹4,000 crore for capacity expansion
articles.summary: Cement manufacturer Dalmia Bharat is looking to raise up to ₹4,000 crore to fund its expansion. The company aims to increase its manufacturing capacity to 110-130 million tonnes per annum by FY31 through acquisitions and new projects. Currently the fourth-largest cement maker in India, it is focusing on scaling its presence across eastern, northeastern, and southern regions.

articles.headline: Russian crude makes up half of India’s oil imports in June
articles.summary: India’s imports of Russian crude oil averaged 2.66 million barrels per day in June, accounting for approximately 50% of the country's total oil purchases. This represents a 40% increase from May, as Indian refiners continue to favor discounted barrels despite US sanctions uncertainty. Russia remains India's largest supplier, followed by the UAE.

articles.headline: Virat Kohli returns to India’s ODI squad for England series
articles.summary: Virat Kohli has been named in India’s 15-man squad for the upcoming three-match ODI series against England. While Yashasvi Jaiswal was dropped, Shubman Gill will lead the side. In another development, Ben Stokes is set to return as England’s Test captain for their match against New Zealand following a disciplinary suspension during the previous game.

articles.headline: Marizanne Kapp’s unbeaten 81 leads South Africa to victory over India
articles.summary: Marizanne Kapp’s brilliant 81 not out off 45 balls helped South Africa chase down 159 to defeat India by six wickets in a women’s T20 match. Despite India's strong start by Smriti Mandhana and Shafali Verma, South Africa’s bowling attack and Kapp’s composed batting ensured a win. The result leaves Group 1 in the T20 World Cup race tied between the two nations.

articles.headline: Indian women’s hockey team wins FIH Nations Cup in Auckland
articles.summary: The Indian women’s hockey team secured the FIH Nations Cup title by defeating hosts New Zealand 2-0 in the final. Goals from Navneet Kaur and Sunelita Toppo ensured an unbeaten run throughout the tournament. With this victory, India has earned a spot in the FIH Pro League for the next season. Hockey India announced a cash prize of ₹3 lakh for each player.

articles.headline: Deniz Undav brace helps Germany beat Ivory Coast in World Cup
articles.summary: Substitute Deniz Undav scored two goals after coming off the bench to secure a 2-1 win for Germany over Ivory Coast. The victory ensures Germany's passage to the World Cup knockout stages for the first time since 2014. Despite being trailing 0-1, Undav’s clinical finishing in the 69th and 94th minutes solved Germany's long-standing search for a reliable centre-forward.

articles.headline: Lamine Yamal scores as Spain secures 4-0 win over Saudi Arabia
articles.summary: 18-year-old sensation Lamine Yamal scored in his first World Cup start as Spain dismantled Saudi Arabia 4-0. Yamal became the eighth youngest scorer in tournament history, touching home a low cross in the 10th minute. The win settled nerves for the Spanish side, which had been held to a goalless draw by Cape Verde in their opening match.

articles.headline: Netherlands crush Sweden 5-1 in World Cup Group F match
articles.summary: The Netherlands delivered a dominant 5-1 performance against Sweden in their final group match. Striker Brian Brobbey and winger Cody Gakpo each scored twice, leading a high-tempo Dutch attack that overwhelmed the Swedish defense. The victory builds significant momentum for Ronald Koeman's side as they head into the knockout rounds.

articles.headline: Curacao earns historic first World Cup point with draw against Ecuador
articles.summary: Curacao achieved a historic milestone by securing its first-ever World Cup point in a 0-0 draw against Ecuador. Goalkeeper Eloy Room was the star of the match, making a record-breaking 15 saves to deny the South American side. The result was celebrated by Dutch royalty and signals the tiny Caribbean nation's growth on the global stage.

articles.headline: Japan beats Tunisia 4-0 in landmark 1,000th World Cup match
articles.summary: Japan marked the 1,000th match in World Cup history with a commanding 4-0 victory over Tunisia. Ayase Ueda scored twice as the Samurai Blue dominated possession and pace. The win ensures Japan’s advancement to the knockout stages for the fourth consecutive time, while Tunisia becomes one of the first teams to be eliminated from the tournament.

articles.headline: Met department denies tornado occurrence in Thoothukudi despite viral videos
articles.summary: The regional meteorological department in Chennai stated that a tornado did not occur in Thoothukudi, contrary to social media claims. Officials explained that viral videos showing a funnel cloud were likely a localized convective vortex or a vigorous updraft from a cumulonimbus cloud. True tornadoes are rare in the region during the southwest monsoon due to the lack of necessary wind shear.

articles.headline: JD Vance references wife Usha and Asim Munir in diplomatic anecdote
articles.summary: US Vice President JD Vance made a light-hearted remark at the Lake Lucerne Summit about the 'two most important people' in his life: his Indian-origin wife, Usha Vance, and Pakistani Army chief Asim Munir. The anecdote went viral online, highlighting the close communication between US and Pakistani officials during ongoing diplomatic negotiations with Iran.

articles.headline: Mitchell Marsh leads Australia to 3-0 series sweep over Bangladesh
articles.summary: Mitchell Marsh smashed 60 runs off 28 balls to propel Australia to a seven-wicket win over Bangladesh, completing a 3-0 T20I series sweep. Australia easily chased the target in Chattogram, showcasing their dominant form ahead of major upcoming tournaments.

articles.headline: Indian men’s hockey team beats Netherlands 3-2 in Pro League
articles.summary: The Indian men's hockey team concluded their Rotterdam leg of the FIH Pro League with a 3-2 victory over the Netherlands. Goals from Jugraj Singh, Abhishek, and Rajinder Singh Yadav secured the win in a closely contested match against the Dutch side.

articles.headline: Iran coach blasts US travel restrictions on World Cup squad
articles.summary: Iran's head coach Amir Ghalenoei criticized US authorities for strict travel restrictions that forced his team to leave the country just hours after each match. He claimed the lack of flexibility damaged his team's on-field performance, though he noted that restrictions might be eased for their final group game in Seattle.

articles.headline: Pink boots trend dominates football World Cup in North America
articles.summary: A striking trend at the current World Cup sees top stars like Gio Reyna, Vinicius Jr, Mbappe, and Harry Kane all wearing pink boots. Major manufacturers produced the footwear for high visibility on the green grass, and the color has become a signature sight for fans across the tournament's diverse venues.

articles.headline: Supreme Court hears plea on environmental regulations
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is hearing a crucial plea concerning the enforcement of environmental regulations. The case involves challenges to industrial clearances and their impact on ecological balance.

articles.headline: AI integration in education to be prioritized
articles.summary: The Ministry of Education plans to prioritize the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in educational institutions. The move aims to enhance learning outcomes and equip students with future-ready skills.

articles.headline: Tech giant unveils new AI-powered platform
articles.summary: A leading technology company has launched a new Artificial Intelligence (AI) powered platform designed to enhance business operations. The platform offers advanced data analytics and automation capabilities.

articles.headline: Government to expand digital infrastructure
articles.summary: The government plans to significantly expand digital infrastructure across the country. This includes increasing internet penetration and promoting digital literacy in rural areas.

articles.headline: AI development to be regulated
articles.summary: The government is considering new regulations for the development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI). The move aims to address ethical concerns and ensure responsible innovation.

articles.headline: Supreme Court asks Centre for updated data on pollution
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has requested the Central government to provide updated data on air pollution levels across major cities. The court emphasized the urgency of addressing environmental concerns.

articles.headline: National education policy review seeks stakeholder feedback
articles.summary: The ongoing review of the national education policy is actively seeking feedback from stakeholders, including educators and students. The aim is to ensure comprehensive and inclusive reforms.

articles.headline: Government launches rural development program
articles.summary: A new rural development program has been launched to enhance infrastructure and livelihood opportunities in villages. The initiative aims to empower local communities and promote sustainable growth.

articles.headline: Parliament debates new cybersecurity bill
articles.summary: Parliament is engaged in debating a new cybersecurity bill aimed at strengthening national digital security. The legislation seeks to address emerging cyber threats and data protection.

articles.headline: Scientists develop new sustainable material
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a novel sustainable material with potential applications in various industries. The material is biodegradable and eco-friendly, offering a greener alternative.

articles.headline: National health survey highlights lifestyle disease burden
articles.summary: A national health survey has highlighted the growing burden of lifestyle-related diseases. The findings emphasize the need for public health interventions and awareness campaigns.

articles.headline: Educational reforms to emphasize critical thinking
articles.summary: Future educational reforms will prioritize the development of critical thinking skills among students. The aim is to foster analytical abilities and problem-solving capabilities.

articles.headline: Global economic forecast revised
articles.summary: The global economic forecast has been revised, with analysts anticipating moderate growth despite ongoing challenges. Key factors influencing the outlook include inflation and geopolitical events.

articles.headline: Parliamentary panel reviews economic policies
articles.summary: A parliamentary panel is reviewing the effectiveness of current economic policies, focusing on growth drivers and fiscal management. The committee seeks to recommend adjustments.

articles.headline: Supreme Court issues directive on child labor
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has issued a directive for stricter enforcement of laws against child labor. The court emphasized the need for proactive measures to eliminate child exploitation.

articles.headline: Supreme Court addresses electoral reforms
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is hearing petitions related to electoral reforms, focusing on issues like campaign finance and voter verification. The court seeks to ensure fair and transparent elections.

articles.headline: Economic growth projected to remain robust
articles.summary: Economic growth is projected to remain robust in the coming quarters, supported by strong domestic demand and policy reforms. The outlook indicates continued expansion.

articles.headline: Scientists make progress in renewable energy storage
articles.summary: Scientists are making significant progress in developing advanced renewable energy storage solutions, focusing on battery technology. The goal is to improve grid stability and energy accessibility.

articles.headline: Supreme Court seeks details on judicial appointments
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has requested detailed information on judicial appointments and vacancies. The court aims to ensure timely filling of positions and efficient functioning of the judiciary.

articles.headline: Cultural heritage sites receive protection status
articles.summary: Several cultural heritage sites have been granted special protection status to preserve their historical significance. The government is implementing conservation measures.

articles.headline: New smart highway project inaugurated
articles.summary: A new smart highway project has been inaugurated, incorporating advanced technology for traffic management and safety. The project aims to improve transportation infrastructure.

articles.headline: Cultural exchange program promotes artistic collaboration
articles.summary: A cultural exchange program is promoting artistic collaboration between artists from different countries. The initiative aims to foster cross-cultural understanding and creative partnerships.

articles.headline: India's economic outlook remains stable
articles.summary: India's economic outlook remains stable, with continued growth expected. Factors like domestic demand and government reforms are supporting the positive trend.

articles.headline: Scientists achieve breakthrough in quantum computing
articles.summary: Scientists have achieved a breakthrough in quantum computing, demonstrating enhanced processing capabilities. This advancement could revolutionize various fields, including medicine and materials science.

articles.headline: Scientists develop AI for weather forecasting
articles.summary: Scientists have developed an advanced AI system for more accurate weather forecasting, improving prediction models. The technology utilizes complex data analysis for better climate insights.

articles.headline: Global health organization warns on rise in chronic diseases
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has warned about the increasing global prevalence of chronic diseases. The organization calls for greater emphasis on prevention and early management.

articles.headline: India-Japan deepen strategic ties
articles.summary: India and Japan are deepening their strategic ties with enhanced cooperation in defense and technology. The partnership aims to promote regional stability and economic growth.

articles.headline: Government approves new urban renewal project
articles.summary: The government has approved a new urban renewal project aimed at revitalizing older city areas. The initiative focuses on infrastructure development and community improvement.

articles.headline: Cultural heritage festival attracts global visitors
articles.summary: A cultural heritage festival is attracting global visitors, showcasing the nation's rich history and traditions. The event promotes cultural tourism and international understanding.

articles.headline: Scientists develop new solar cell technology
articles.summary: Scientists have developed a new, more efficient solar cell technology, significantly improving energy conversion rates. This breakthrough could accelerate the adoption of solar power.

articles.headline: Over 4,000 Bengaluru prison inmates participate in mass Yoga Day session
articles.summary: More than 4,000 inmates and staff at the Central Prison, Parappana Agrahara, participated in a mass yoga session for the 12th International Yoga Day. The event, supported by the Art of Living and UCO Bank, focused on the theme 'Yoga for Healthy Ageing.' Prison officials emphasized that yoga helps promote mental strength, self-confidence, and a disciplined lifestyle among the incarcerated.
